message:
The other day, I found myself in a bit of a pickle. It was a typical Tuesday, and suddenly the power went out. My kids were in the middle of a Lego-building marathon, and I could see the panic start to set in as the room went dark. But then I remembered—we had our go-bags packed and ready! I quickly pulled out some flashlights and snacks from the bag, and we turned it into an impromptu indoor camping night. The kids loved it, and we shared stories by flashlight until the power came back on. 

That little bit of preparedness transformed what could have been a stressful situation into a fun family memory. Sometimes, it’s the small wins that really save the day! Have you had any experiences where being prepared turned things around for you?
